standard_frameworks.name: EU Standard Contractual Clauses (2021/914)
standard_frameworks.description: Mandatory framework for transferring personal data from EU/EEA to third countries without an adequacy decision, replacing the 2010 SCCs.

standard_frameworks.name: UK International Data Transfer Agreement (IDTA)
standard_frameworks.description: UK replacement for EU SCCs following Brexit, providing a standalone framework for international personal data transfers from the UK.

standard_frameworks.name: UK International Data Transfer Addendum to EU SCCs
standard_frameworks.description: UK addendum that can be appended to EU SCCs to make them valid for UK data transfers, providing an alternative to the standalone IDTA.

standard_frameworks.name: EDPB Guidelines on Processor Agreements (07/2020)
standard_frameworks.description: Guidelines on concepts of controller and processor under GDPR, providing practical guidance on structuring data processing agreements.

standard_frameworks.name: EDPB Recommendations on Supplementary Measures (01/2020)
standard_frameworks.description: Recommendations on supplementary measures to supplement transfer tools to ensure compliance with EU personal data protection standards following Schrems II.

standard_frameworks.name: EU AI Act Contractual Requirements (2024/1689)
standard_frameworks.description: Contractual obligations arising from the EU AI Act for providers, deployers, importers, and distributors of AI systems across risk categories.

standard_frameworks.name: EU Cyber Resilience Act Supply Chain Requirements
standard_frameworks.description: Contractual requirements for products with digital elements regarding cybersecurity, vulnerability handling, SBOM provision, and security update obligations.

standard_frameworks.name: DORA ICT Third-Party Risk Management Framework
standard_frameworks.description: Mandatory contractual provisions for financial entities outsourcing ICT services under the Digital Operational Resilience Act, including critical provider requirements.

standard_frameworks.name: ICC Incoterms 2020
standard_frameworks.description: International Commercial Terms defining the responsibilities of sellers and buyers for the delivery of goods under sales contracts, covering 11 trade terms.

standard_frameworks.name: UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods (CISG)
standard_frameworks.description: International treaty governing contracts for the international sale of goods between parties in different contracting states. Applies by default unless expressly excluded.
standard_frameworks.authority: United Nations Commission on International Trade Law

standard_frameworks.name: UNIDROIT Principles of International Commercial Contracts (2016)
standard_frameworks.description: Non-binding restatement of general principles for international commercial contracts, widely used as a guide for contract interpretation and gap-filling.
standard_frameworks.authority: International Institute for the Unification of Private Law

standard_frameworks.name: ICC Model Contract for International Sale of Goods
standard_frameworks.description: Model contract providing standard terms for international sale of manufactured goods, incorporating Incoterms and international trade best practices.

standard_frameworks.name: ICC Force Majeure and Hardship Clauses 2020
standard_frameworks.description: Updated model force majeure and hardship clauses that can be incorporated into any contract, addressing pandemic and cyber-attack scenarios.

standard_frameworks.name: UNCITRAL Model Law on International Commercial Arbitration
standard_frameworks.description: Model law adopted by many jurisdictions providing a framework for international commercial arbitration, including arbitral procedure, awards, and enforcement.
standard_frameworks.authority: United Nations Commission on International Trade Law

standard_frameworks.name: Hague Convention on Choice of Court Agreements (2005)
standard_frameworks.description: International convention providing rules for the enforcement of exclusive choice of court agreements in international civil and commercial matters.
standard_frameworks.authority: Hague Conference on Private International Law

standard_frameworks.name: ISDA Master Agreement (2002)
standard_frameworks.description: Standard master agreement for over-the-counter derivatives transactions, providing the contractual framework for bilateral trading relationships.

standard_frameworks.name: ISDA Credit Support Annex (CSA)
standard_frameworks.description: Standardized collateral agreement for OTC derivatives, specifying credit support obligations, eligible collateral, and margin requirements.

standard_frameworks.name: LMA Facility Agreement
standard_frameworks.description: Standard form facility agreement for syndicated lending published by the Loan Market Association, widely used in European leveraged and investment grade lending.

standard_frameworks.name: BAFT Master Participation Agreement
standard_frameworks.description: Standard master agreement for trade finance risk distribution, enabling banks to participate in trade finance assets through funded and unfunded sub-participations.
standard_frameworks.authority: Bankers Association for Finance and Trade

standard_frameworks.name: NIST SP 800-161r1 Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
standard_frameworks.description: Guidance for identifying, assessing, and mitigating cybersecurity supply chain risks, including contractual requirements for ICT/OT suppliers.
standard_frameworks.authority: National Institute of Standards and Technology

standard_frameworks.name: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
standard_frameworks.description: Voluntary framework consisting of standards, guidelines, and best practices for managing cybersecurity risk, including supply chain risk management category.
standard_frameworks.authority: National Institute of Standards and Technology

standard_frameworks.name: CISA Secure by Design Principles
standard_frameworks.description: Principles for technology manufacturers to build security into products from the design phase, with contractual implications for procurement.

standard_frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Supplier Relationship Controls
standard_frameworks.description: Information security management system standard including Annex A controls A.5.19-A.5.23 for supplier relationships and cloud service security.

standard_frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 27701:2019 Privacy Information Management
standard_frameworks.description: Extension to ISO 27001/27002 for privacy information management, providing guidance for PII controllers and processors with contractual requirements.

standard_frameworks.name: SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria
standard_frameworks.description: Trust services criteria for evaluating controls relevant to security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy in service organizations.
standard_frameworks.authority: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants

standard_frameworks.name: CSA STAR (Security, Trust, Assurance, and Risk)
standard_frameworks.description: Cloud security assurance program providing multiple levels of assurance through self-assessment, third-party audit, and continuous monitoring.

standard_frameworks.name: OWASP Software Assurance Maturity Model (SAMM)
standard_frameworks.description: Framework for formulating and implementing a software security strategy, with contractual implications for secure development lifecycle requirements.

standard_frameworks.name: FAR Part 27 - Patents, Data, and Copyrights
standard_frameworks.description: Federal Acquisition Regulation provisions governing intellectual property rights in government contracts, including data rights, patent rights, and copyright allocation.

standard_frameworks.name: FAR 52.227-14 Rights in Data - General
standard_frameworks.description: Standard FAR clause defining government rights in technical data and computer software delivered under government contracts, with limited rights and restricted rights categories.

standard_frameworks.name: DFARS 252.227-7013 Rights in Technical Data - Noncommercial
standard_frameworks.description: Defense-specific clause defining government rights in noncommercial technical data, with unlimited rights, government purpose rights, and limited rights categories.

standard_frameworks.name: DFARS 252.227-7014 Rights in Noncommercial Computer Software
standard_frameworks.description: Defense-specific clause defining government rights in noncommercial computer software and documentation, with restricted rights protections for contractor-developed software.

standard_frameworks.name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) 2.0
standard_frameworks.description: DoD program requiring defense contractors to achieve and maintain cybersecurity certification at levels commensurate with the sensitivity of controlled unclassified information.

standard_frameworks.name: FAR Part 12 - Acquisition of Commercial Products and Services
standard_frameworks.description: FAR provisions for acquiring commercial products and services with streamlined procedures and commercial item IP protections.

standard_frameworks.name: SBIR/STTR Data Rights Protection
standard_frameworks.description: Data rights protections for Small Business Innovation Research and Small Business Technology Transfer programs, providing extended protection periods for developed technologies.

standard_frameworks.name: FAR 52.227-11 Patent Rights - Ownership by Contractor
standard_frameworks.description: Standard patent rights clause implementing Bayh-Dole Act, allowing contractors to retain title to inventions made under government contracts with government license rights.

standard_frameworks.name: EDPB Binding Corporate Rules for Controllers (WP256)
standard_frameworks.description: Framework for multinational organizations to adopt legally binding internal rules permitting intra-group transfers of personal data outside the EU/EEA, serving as an alternative to SCCs for controller-to-controller transfers.

standard_frameworks.name: EDPB Binding Corporate Rules for Processors (WP257)
standard_frameworks.description: Framework enabling processor organizations with global operations to implement binding internal rules for processing personal data transferred from EU/EEA clients, with commitments enforceable by data subjects.

standard_frameworks.name: EU Commission Art. 28 GDPR DPA Model Clauses
standard_frameworks.description: Standard contractual clauses for data processing agreements under Article 28(7) GDPR, providing a harmonized template for controller-processor contracts within the EU/EEA.

standard_frameworks.name: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 Security and Privacy Controls
standard_frameworks.description: Comprehensive catalog of security and privacy controls for federal information systems and organizations, widely referenced in vendor contracts to define baseline security requirements and audit expectations.
standard_frameworks.authority: National Institute of Standards and Technology

standard_frameworks.name: CIS Critical Security Controls v8
standard_frameworks.description: Prioritized set of 18 cybersecurity safeguards mapped to implementation groups, commonly referenced in outsourcing and managed services contracts to define minimum security baselines.

standard_frameworks.name: HIPAA Business Associate Agreement Requirements
standard_frameworks.description: Mandatory contractual requirements under 45 CFR 164.504(e) for covered entities engaging business associates who create, receive, maintain, or transmit protected health information.
standard_frameworks.authority: US Department of Health and Human Services

standard_frameworks.name: Basel III/IV Outsourcing Principles (BCBS239)
standard_frameworks.description: Principles for sound management of operational risk in outsourcing arrangements by banks, including contractual requirements for third-party risk management, data security, and business continuity.

standard_frameworks.name: MiFID II Outsourcing Delegation Requirements
standard_frameworks.description: Regulatory requirements under MiFID II and MiFIR for investment firms delegating critical or important operational functions, including mandatory contractual terms for oversight, audit, and termination.

standard_frameworks.name: EBA Guidelines on Outsourcing Arrangements (EBA/GL/2019/02)
standard_frameworks.description: Comprehensive guidelines for credit institutions and payment institutions on outsourcing arrangements, defining mandatory contractual provisions for critical and important functions.

standard_frameworks.name: FIDIC Red Book (2017) - Conditions of Contract for Construction
standard_frameworks.description: Internationally recognized standard form contract for construction works designed by the employer, covering measurement-based contracts with engineer administration and detailed provisions for variations, claims, and disputes.

standard_frameworks.name: FIDIC Yellow Book (2017) - Conditions of Contract for Plant and Design-Build
standard_frameworks.description: Standard form contract for projects where the contractor designs and builds, including detailed provisions for design obligations, testing, and performance guarantees for plant and equipment.

standard_frameworks.name: NEC4 Engineering and Construction Contract
standard_frameworks.description: Collaborative construction contract widely mandated in UK public sector procurement, featuring early warning mechanisms, compensation events, and proactive project management through mutual trust and cooperation.

standard_frameworks.name: JCT Design and Build Contract (DB 2024)
standard_frameworks.description: Standard form UK construction contract for design-and-build procurement, where the contractor is responsible for both design and construction with a lump sum price, widely used in private sector UK projects.

standard_frameworks.name: ILO Declaration on Fundamental Principles and Rights at Work
standard_frameworks.description: International labour standards declaration covering freedom of association, elimination of forced labour, abolition of child labour, elimination of discrimination, and safe working environment, increasingly referenced in supply chain and employment contracts.

standard_frameworks.name: OECD Principles on Artificial Intelligence (2024 Update)
standard_frameworks.description: Updated principles for trustworthy AI covering inclusive growth, human-centred values, transparency, robustness, and accountability, increasingly incorporated into AI procurement contracts and technology partnership agreements.
standard_frameworks.authority: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development

standard_frameworks.name: IEEE 7000-2021 Standard for Ethical AI System Design
standard_frameworks.description: Standard establishing processes for addressing ethical concerns during system design, providing a methodology for embedding values into autonomous and intelligent systems through stakeholder analysis and value prioritization.
standard_frameworks.authority: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers

standard_frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 AI Management System
standard_frameworks.description: Management system standard for organizations providing or using AI systems, specifying requirements for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and improving an AI management system within the context of the organization.

standard_frameworks.name: ISO 28000:2022 Supply Chain Security Management
standard_frameworks.description: Security management system standard for the supply chain, specifying requirements for a security management system including aspects critical to security assurance of supply chain operations and their contractual underpinnings.

standard_frameworks.name: ISO 14001:2015 Environmental Management Systems
standard_frameworks.description: Environmental management system standard providing a framework for organizations to manage environmental responsibilities, increasingly required in procurement contracts and supply chain agreements as evidence of environmental commitment.

standard_frameworks.name: ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management Systems
standard_frameworks.description: Quality management system standard specifying requirements for consistent provision of products and services that meet customer and regulatory requirements, commonly referenced as a baseline supplier qualification criterion in commercial contracts.

standard_frameworks.name: IATF 16949:2016 Automotive Quality Management
standard_frameworks.description: Quality management system standard for the automotive industry combining ISO 9001 with additional automotive-specific requirements, mandatory for direct and sub-tier suppliers to major automotive OEMs.

standard_frameworks.name: CSA Cloud Controls Matrix v4
standard_frameworks.description: Cybersecurity control framework for cloud computing composed of 197 control objectives across 17 domains, used in cloud service contracts to specify security requirements and assess provider compliance.

standard_frameworks.name: EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD)
standard_frameworks.description: Directive requiring large and listed companies to report on sustainability matters using European Sustainability Reporting Standards, with contractual implications for supply chain data collection and value chain due diligence.

standard_frameworks.name: EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD)
standard_frameworks.description: Directive requiring companies to conduct human rights and environmental due diligence across their value chains, with mandatory contractual cascading of obligations to direct and indirect business partners.

standard_frameworks.name: PCI DSS v4.0.1 Third-Party Service Provider Requirements
standard_frameworks.description: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard requirements applicable to service providers handling cardholder data, including mandatory contractual provisions for data security, incident response, and compliance validation.
standard_frameworks.authority: Payment Card Industry Security Standards Council

standard_frameworks.name: SWIFT Customer Security Programme (CSP)
standard_frameworks.description: Mandatory security framework for institutions connected to the SWIFT network, requiring contractual commitments to implement security controls, undergo independent assessment, and attest compliance annually.
standard_frameworks.authority: Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication

standard_frameworks.name: ISO 22301:2019 Business Continuity Management
standard_frameworks.description: Standard for business continuity management systems specifying requirements to plan, establish, implement, operate, monitor, review, maintain, and continually improve a documented management system for business disruption protection.

standard_frameworks.name: ISO 31000:2018 Risk Management
standard_frameworks.description: International standard providing principles, framework, and process for managing risk across all types of organizations, referenced in outsourcing contracts and partnership agreements to establish risk management expectations.

standard_frameworks.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0
standard_frameworks.description: Framework for managing risks associated with AI systems throughout their lifecycle, providing guidance on governance, mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks increasingly referenced in AI procurement and vendor contracts.
standard_frameworks.authority: National Institute of Standards and Technology

standard_frameworks.name: ETSI EN 303 645 Consumer IoT Cybersecurity
standard_frameworks.description: European standard for cybersecurity baseline requirements for consumer Internet of Things devices, with contractual implications for manufacturers regarding default passwords, software updates, vulnerability disclosure, and secure communication.

standard_frameworks.name: ITU-T X.805 Security Architecture for Telecom Systems
standard_frameworks.description: ITU Telecommunication Standardization Sector security architecture providing an end-to-end framework for network security, referenced in telecommunications procurement and interconnection agreements.

standard_frameworks.name: NIS2 Directive Supply Chain Requirements (2022/2555)
standard_frameworks.description: Directive imposing cybersecurity risk management and supply chain security obligations on essential and important entities, requiring contractual provisions for ICT supply chain transparency, incident reporting, and security measures.

standard_frameworks.name: UK Modern Slavery Act 2015 - Supply Chain Provisions
standard_frameworks.description: Legislation requiring commercial organizations with turnover above GBP 36 million to publish annual transparency statements on steps taken to ensure modern slavery and human trafficking are not occurring in their supply chains.

standard_frameworks.name: UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights
standard_frameworks.description: Authoritative global standard for preventing and addressing human rights impacts linked to business activity, establishing the Protect, Respect and Remedy framework increasingly embedded in supply chain and procurement contracts.

standard_frameworks.name: EU Deforestation-Free Products Regulation (2023/1115)
standard_frameworks.description: Regulation requiring operators and traders placing specific commodities on the EU market to ensure products are deforestation-free through due diligence statements, with contractual cascading requirements through the supply chain.

standard_frameworks.name: ISO 37001:2016 Anti-Bribery Management Systems
standard_frameworks.description: Standard specifying requirements for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and improving an anti-bribery management system, referenced in international joint venture, agency, and distribution contracts to demonstrate anti-corruption commitment.

standard_frameworks.name: ICC Arbitration Rules (2021)
standard_frameworks.description: Comprehensive procedural rules for international commercial arbitration administered by the ICC International Court of Arbitration, governing the conduct of arbitral proceedings from request through award.

standard_frameworks.name: LCIA Arbitration Rules (2020)
standard_frameworks.description: Rules of the London Court of International Arbitration providing a procedural framework for administered international arbitration, commonly selected in contracts governed by English law or involving London-based parties.

standard_frameworks.name: SCC Arbitration Rules (2023)
standard_frameworks.description: Rules of the Arbitration Institute of the Stockholm Chamber of Commerce, widely used as a neutral forum in East-West trade disputes and in contracts involving parties from different legal traditions.
standard_frameworks.authority: Stockholm Chamber of Commerce Arbitration Institute

standard_frameworks.name: FedRAMP Authorization Requirements for Cloud Services
standard_frameworks.description: Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program providing a standardized approach to security authorization for cloud products and services used by US federal agencies, with mandatory contractual security and compliance requirements.

standard_frameworks.name: TISAX Trusted Information Security Assessment Exchange
standard_frameworks.description: Automotive industry standard for information security assessment based on the VDA ISA questionnaire, required by European automotive OEMs for suppliers handling confidential prototypes, connected car data, or personal data.

standard_frameworks.name: EU Conflict Minerals Regulation (2017/821)
standard_frameworks.description: Regulation establishing supply chain due diligence obligations for EU importers of tin, tantalum, tungsten, and gold originating from conflict-affected and high-risk areas, with contractual cascading of due diligence requirements.

standard_frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 27018:2019 Protection of PII in Public Clouds
standard_frameworks.description: Code of practice for protection of personally identifiable information in public cloud computing environments acting as PII processors, commonly referenced in cloud service agreements to supplement data processing terms.

standard_frameworks.name: Gaia-X Federation Services and Trust Framework
standard_frameworks.description: European initiative establishing rules for interoperable and trustworthy data exchange, defining contractual standards for data sovereignty, portability, and self-descriptions in federated cloud and data space ecosystems.
standard_frameworks.authority: Gaia-X European Association for Data and Cloud

standard_frameworks.name: EU Data Act Contractual Fairness Rules (2023/2854)
standard_frameworks.description: Regulation establishing rules on fair access to and use of data, including provisions on unfair contractual terms in B2B data sharing, switching between data processing services, and interoperability requirements.

standard_frameworks.name: Global Cross-Border Privacy Rules (CBPR) System
standard_frameworks.description: International certification framework for cross-border data transfers enabling organizations to demonstrate compliance with internationally recognized data privacy protections, serving as an alternative mechanism to SCCs for APAC-region transfers.
